title: The Top 10 Animated Movies of 2022 and ‘Guillermo del Toro’s Pinocchio’
format: Sean and Charles Holmes spend a whole episode on animation: why 'Guillermo del Toro's Pinocchio' beats the Zemeckis one, why Turning Red got dumped straight to Disney+, and why nobody in America wants to treat cartoons as a real medium. Then they run their own top-five lists, which flip at the top.

segments.heading: Guillermo del Toro's Pinocchio
segments.note: stop motion, fascism, and the other Pinocchio

films.title: Guillermo del Toro's Pinocchio
films.note: Fourteen years getting made. Del Toro's stop-motion version of Collodi's story folds in Mussolini-era fascism and body horror; Ron Perlman, Ewan McGregor, David Bradley, Gregory Mann, and Burn Gorman voice it.

films.title: Pinocchio
films.note: Zemeckis's same-year digitally animated version with Tom Hanks. Called one of the year's worst misfires.

films.title: Wendell & Wild
films.note: Another Netflix stop-motion release, cited as sharing the format's cramped-runtime problem.

films.title: Avatar: The Way of Water
films.note: Comparison point: visuals so committed they carry the movie past a thin story, same as Pinocchio.

films.title: Pan's Labyrinth
films.note: Cited as the template for Del Toro's fascism-and-fairy-tale instincts.

films.title: The Devil's Backbone
films.note: Same point: Del Toro's authoritarianism obsession goes back decades.

segments.heading: The state of the animation business in 2022
segments.note: streaming dumps, IP fatigue, and who's actually winning

films.title: Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse
films.note: Credited with breaking animation out of the house Pixar-eyes style.

films.title: The Bad Guys
films.note: DreamWorks theatrical release that looks nothing like a Pixar knockoff, and did well in theaters.

films.title: Puss in Boots: The Last Wish
films.note: Another DreamWorks title cited alongside The Bad Guys as evidence the studio's house style is loosening up.

films.title: The Mitchells vs. the Machines
films.note: Cited as part of the same wave of studios moving past the default Pixar look.

films.title: Strange World
films.note: Disney's original sci-fi adventure, described as an outright box-office failure.

films.title: Lightyear
films.note: Also flopped in theaters; became a hit with kids only once it hit Disney+.

films.title: Coco
films.note: Called out as the last Pixar original (non-sequel) to be a genuine cultural hit.

films.title: Incredibles 2
films.note: A sequel success, contrasted with Pixar's thinner run of original-story hits.

films.title: Minions: The Rise of Gru
films.note: Illumination's big 2022 win, credited with holding onto a teenage audience through the 'gentleminions' craze.

films.title: The Super Mario Bros. Movie
films.note: Discussed off the trailers ahead of its 2023 release; Jack Black plays Bowser.

films.title: Wreck-It Ralph
films.note: Reference point for the video-game-movie idea Mario is chasing.

films.title: The Lego Movie
films.note: Blamed for kicking off the wave of winking, meta animated comedies.

films.title: Who Framed Roger Rabbit
films.note: Held up as the gold standard for blending classic-cartoon characters into a modern story, a bar Chip 'n Dale doesn't clear.

films.title: Chip 'n Dale: Rescue Rangers
films.note: Akiva Schaffer's Disney+ release, with John Mulaney and Andy Samberg voicing Chip and Dale.

films.title: One Piece Film: Red
films.note: Discussed as an example of anime tentpoles reliably drawing a core U.S. audience to theaters.

films.title: Dragon Ball Super: Super Hero
films.note: Cited alongside One Piece Film: Red as part of the same anime-in-theaters trend.

segments.heading: The coming-of-age wave, plus Mad God and Belle

films.title: Apollo 10½: A Space Age Childhood
films.note: Richard Linklater's rotoscoped memory piece about a kid obsessed with NASA in 1960s Houston, his third rotoscope film.

films.title: Waking Life
films.note: Linklater's earlier rotoscope film.

films.title: A Scanner Darkly
films.note: Linklater's other rotoscope film.

films.title: Dazed and Confused
films.note: Cited as the coming-of-age movie that does work for Charles, unlike Apollo 10½.

films.title: The Fabelmans
films.note: Named as part of the year's glut of directors making their own childhood-memory movie.

films.title: Bardo, False Chronicle of a Handful of Truths
films.note: Same wave: filmmaker autobiography.

films.title: Aftersun
films.note: Same wave: filmmaker autobiography.

films.title: Belle
films.note: Mamoru Hosoda's anime retelling of Beauty and the Beast set inside a metaverse-style virtual world.

films.title: Mad God
films.note: Phil Tippett's decades-in-the-making stop-motion descent into hell: 83 minutes, punishing to watch, but a real feat.

films.title: Phil Tippett: Mad Dreams and Monsters
films.note: Documentary about Tippett's decades making Mad God, recommended as a companion watch.

films.title: The Sea Beast
films.note: Directed by Chris Williams (Bolt, Big Hero 6, Moana co-director); a Netflix action-adventure that plays as a fake-news allegory.

films.title: Luck
films.note: Apple's animated release, mentioned in passing as part of streamers moving into original animation.

films.title: Guillermo del Toro's Pinocchio
films.note: No. 1: "I would buy frames of Pinocchio and hang it up in my household."

films.title: Turning Red
films.note: No. 2: more rewatchable than Pinocchio, but Pinocchio wins as pure image.

films.title: Belle
films.note: No. 3: "exactly what I want from anime."

films.title: Jujutsu Kaisen 0
films.note: No. 4: included partly just to get the title said on the show.

films.title: The Sea Beast
films.note: No. 5: an action-forward Netflix film that didn't talk down to kids.

films.title: Turning Red
films.note: No. 1: more fun at the movies than Pinocchio, even if it's less of a visual achievement.

films.title: Guillermo del Toro's Pinocchio
films.note: No. 2: "a gobsmacking achievement of animation."

films.title: Mad God
films.note: No. 3: "not a fun watch per se" but impossible not to acknowledge.

films.title: Apollo 10½: A Space Age Childhood
films.note: No. 4

films.title: Chip 'n Dale: Rescue Rangers
films.note: No. 5: "our Who Framed Roger Rabbit," not as good as the original but the closest thing since.

Puss in Boots: The Last Wish.title: Puss in Boots: The Last Wish

Bardo, False Chronicle of a Handful of T.title: BARDO, False Chronicle of a Handful of Truths

Phil Tippett: Mad Dreams and Monsters.title: Phil Tippett: Mad Dreams and Monsters
